# Result handling

> Every method returns a discriminated union and never throws — check error, then narrow.

Every method returns a discriminated union; the SDK catches network, JSON, and HTTP errors internally:

```ts
type EmailSdkResult<T> =
  | { data: T; error: null }
  | { data: null; error: { message: string } };
```

So **no try/catch around SDK calls** — check `error` and narrow:

```ts
const { data, error } = await client.send({
  /* … */
});

if (error) {
  // error.message is the API's message
  return;
}

data.id; // non-null here — TypeScript narrows after the early return
```

After `if (error)` returns or throws, `data` is non-null. Don’t add fallbacks for thrown SDK errors — they can’t happen.
